message:
fix: tombstone-based GC, unwrap elimination, and drain-based timer processing

Four targeted bug fixes across different subsystems:

- rate_limit.rs: Replace Vec::remove() in the token bucket with a
  tombstone-based GC pattern using RejectionReason::Consumed. The old
  approach shifted indices during iteration causing O(n²) worst-case
  and potential skipped entries; tombstones defer compaction to a
  periodic sweep pass

- quic/endpoint.rs: Eliminate unwrap() in server() by pattern-matching
  the cert/key pair upfront. Previously, missing TLS configuration
  would panic at runtime; now returns a descriptive error before
  attempting to bind the QUIC listener

- time/sleep.rs: Guard timer_handle.take() with if-let instead of
  unwrap(). A sleep future that is dropped and then polled (possible
  in select! branches) would panic on the bare unwrap; the if-let
  safely no-ops

- time/wheel.rs: Replace the swap_remove-in-a-loop pattern with
  drain() for expired timer processing. swap_remove inside a forward
  loop silently skips the element swapped into the current index;
  drain() processes all entries correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
